name: Naglfar
description: Naglfar is a ship in Norse mythology constructed entirely from the fingernails and toenails of the dead. Its creation is a grim and unsettling process, fueled by the negligence of the living who fail to properly dispose of the dead's nail clippings. The accumulation of these macabre components over time gradually forms the hull and structure of this terrifying vessel. Naglfar is prophesied to sail forth during Ragnarök, the end of the world, carrying an army of the dead led by the giant Hrym. This signifies the ultimate triumph of chaos and destruction over order and life. Its very existence serves as a chilling reminder of the importance of respecting the dead and the potential consequences of neglecting even seemingly insignificant rituals. The ship's gruesome composition and its role in the apocalyptic battle make it one of the most horrifying and iconic images in Norse mythology, symbolizing the inevitable decay and ultimate demise of all things. The size of Naglfar is never explicitly stated, but it is implied to be large enough to carry a substantial army, further emphasizing its terrifying power. The act of clipping nails and toenails is a necessary chore in life, but in the world of Norse mythology, it is a constant reminder of mortality and the potential for even the smallest actions to have catastrophic consequences. The ship’s appearance heralds the beginning of the end, a physical manifestation of the doom that awaits the gods and mankind alike. Therefore, proper disposal of nail clippings was a serious concern in Norse society, a practice intended to delay the inevitable arrival of Naglfar and the onset of Ragnarök.
subtitle: The Ship of Nails: Harbinger of Ragnarök
material: Fingernails and toenails of the dead

keyMyths.title: Construction of Naglfar
keyMyths.description: Naglfar is built from the untrimmed nails of the dead. Failing to dispose of nail clippings properly hastens its completion and the coming of Ragnarök.

keyMyths.title: Naglfar's Role in Ragnarök
keyMyths.description: During Ragnarök, Naglfar will sail from the east, carrying giants and the dead to Vigrid, where the final battle will be fought against the gods.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, detailing the creation and role of Naglfar.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, detailing the creation and role of Naglfar.
